common.name: Name
common.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}?

login.title: Sign in
login.breakGlassWarning: Local break-glass admin login — use only when SAML is unavailable.

deviceGroups.title: Device groups
deviceGroups.subtitle: Flat groupings of network devices. Each device belongs to exactly one group.
deviceGroups.emptyState: No device groups yet. Create one to start authorising users.

privilegeProfiles.title: Privilege profiles
privilegeProfiles.subtitle: Reusable bundles of TACACS+ authorisation attributes. Service is implicitly shell.
privilegeProfiles.denyWinsHint: Deny patterns take precedence over permit patterns; both are PCRE.
privilegeProfiles.extraAvPairsHint: Additional AVs set on the shell session, e.g. idletime=30.

devices.title: Devices
devices.subtitle: Network devices that talk TACACS+ to us. Longest-prefix match resolves NAS IP to a device.
devices.secretCreateHint: Stored encrypted. Provide later via rotate-secret if you want to skip for now.
devices.rotateHint: The current secret moves into the previous slot; the new one becomes active.
devices.emptyState: No devices yet. Add at least one to start receiving TACACS+ traffic.

effective.title: Effective permissions
effective.subtitle: For a chosen user, the winning Authorization per DeviceGroup (per ADR-0006: direct-user beats AD-group; otherwise highest priv-lvl wins). Overridden candidates are shown for traceability.
effective.noUserSelected: Select a user to see their effective permissions.
effective.emptyState: This user has no matching Authorization on any DeviceGroup.

audit.title: Audit log
audit.subtitle: Append-only history of Web-UI auth events, CRUD changes, and live TACACS+ authn/authz from MAVIS. Admin-only.
audit.empty: No audit entries match the current filters.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Runtime configuration. Changes take effect immediately and are recorded in the audit log.
settings.ldapDescription: Endpoint MAVIS binds to for live password verification. Use the ldaps:// scheme for TLS.
settings.ldapEmptyHint: Not configured. The MAVIS AUTH path returns ERR (fail-closed) until an endpoint is set.
settings.webDescription: Canonical URL operators reach this UI on. Used for absolute links and (later) SAML callbacks.
settings.webEmptyHint: Falls back to the BASE_URL environment variable until configured here.
settings.tlsDescription: The cert nginx serves on the HTTPS listener. Changes take effect on the next nginx container restart.
settings.tlsRestartHint: Restart the nginx container after updating: docker compose restart nginx
settings.tlsPfxHint: PKCS#12 file from Windows AD CS or any tool that exports cert + key + chain into a single password-protected .pfx. Intermediates land alongside the leaf so browsers can walk the chain.
settings.tlsPfxNoPasswordHint: Leave empty for an unencrypted PFX.
settings.samlDescription: Federate admin login to an external IdP (e.g. FortiAuthenticator). Operators map AD-group claims to roles (admin / operator / viewer).
settings.samlUnconfiguredHint: Not configured yet. Generate the SP keypair, import the IdP metadata, define at least one role mapping.
settings.samlImportHint: Paste the metadata XML or pick a file. The IdP entity ID, SSO URL, and signing cert are extracted automatically.
settings.samlMappingDescription: Match the SAML attribute carrying group claims; first mapping wins.
settings.samlGroupAttributeHint: Default memberOf. Some IdPs use a custom claim URI.
settings.syncDescription: Service account that periodically pulls users + groups from AD into the local DB so they appear in the Authorisations editor.
settings.syncUnconfiguredHint: Not configured yet. Fill in the bind DN, password, and at least one base DN to enable.
settings.syncMissingLdapUrlHint: The LDAPS endpoint URL has to be set in the "Active Directory / LDAPS" card above before the sync worker can connect.
settings.syncBindPasswordSetHint: Leave empty to keep the stored password.
settings.syncBaseDnsHint: One DN per line; paged subtree search runs against each.
settings.syncLastRunOk: Last run {{when}} — {{seen}} seen, {{inserted}} new, {{updated}} updated, {{disabled}} disabled

authorizations.title: Authorizations
authorizations.subtitle: Edges binding a principal (user or AD group) to a device group + privilege profile.
authorizations.emptyState: No authorizations yet. The Effective-Permissions view will be empty until you add at least one.
